channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Community Pantry Supports Nova Scotia Veterans Coping with Rising Living Expenses
item.description: In Nova Scotia, a local community pantry has been established to support veterans who are struggling with the rising cost of living. Many former service members face financial challenges due to inflat...

item.title: Nkosi Champions Early Childhood Development and Inclusive Fatherhood in South Africa
item.description: Siphesihle Perfect Nkosi is making significant strides in South Africa’s Early Childhood Development (ECD) sector through his initiative, Isiqalo. Focused on under-resourced communities, Isiqalo equip...

item.title: Daikin Launches Air Creator Lineup for Personalized Home Air Comfort
item.description: Daikin Philippines has introduced its new residential Air Creator lineup, aimed at giving users full control over their indoor air environment. The lineup includes three main products: the Air Creator...

item.title: President Tinubu Departs Abuja for State Visit to United Kingdom Hosted by King Charles III
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu, together with im wife di First Lady Senator Oluremi Tinubu, don comot Abuja on Tuesday, March 17, 2026, to begin one historic two-day state visit to di United Kingdom. Dis...

item.title: Nvidia Forecasts $1 Trillion in Orders for Its Blackwell and Vera Rubin AI Chips
item.description: During Nvidia’s 2026 GTC Conference in San Jose, CEO Jensen Huang announced that combined orders for the company’s Blackwell and upcoming Vera Rubin chip architectures are projected to reach $1 trilli...

item.title: Interactive Map of Key Damage Sites from US-Israel Strikes on Iran
item.description: This Wall Street Journal live coverage card features an interactive map detailing sites in Iran damaged during recent US and Israeli military strikes amid escalating Middle East conflict as of March 2...

item.title: Trump Makes Controversial Remarks About U.S. Authority Over Cuba
item.description: On March 16, 2026, former President Donald Trump made highly controversial comments regarding Cuba during a press interaction, stating that he could 'do anything' with the Caribbean nation. His remark...

item.title: Chelsea to Face PSG Without Several Key Players Including Reece James
item.description: Chelsea are set for a tough Champions League clash against Paris Saint-Germain, trailing 5-2 from the first leg. The Blues face a big challenge as captain Reece James is sidelined with a hamstring inj...

item.title: DOJ Transmits Malversation Complaints Against Villanueva to Ombudsman for Review
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has formally transmitted its findings on three malversation complaints against Senator Joel Villanueva to the Office of the Ombudsman. The complaints are connected to a...

item.title: Public Opinion Mixed on Starmer's Handling of Iran Crisis and Energy Costs
item.description: Recent polling by YouGov for Sky News indicates that the UK public is divided over Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer's handling of the Iran conflict. While 41% of voters believe he has responded poorly ...

item.title: Senate Secretariat clarifies Land Cruiser purchase for Chairman Gilani
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Senate Secretariat has clarified reports regarding the purchase of a Land Cruiser for Senate Chairman Yousaf Raza Gilani, stating that the acquisition was completed in May 2025 under th...

item.title: Venezuela defeats Italy to reach World Baseball Classic final against Team USA
item.description: Venezuela secured a historic 4-2 victory over Italy in the semifinal of the 2026 World Baseball Classic, earning a spot in the championship game against Team USA. The Venezuelan team overcame a one-ru...

item.title: Government Urges Judicious LPG Use Amid Supply Concerns from Middle East Conflict, Assures No Panic Needed for Domestic Consumers
item.description: The Government of India has described the LPG supply situation as a 'matter of concern' due to ongoing disruptions from the conflict in West Asia, particularly affecting imports through the Strait of ...

item.title: Multiple Suicide Bombings Kill 23, Injure Over 100 in Maiduguri

item.title: Sindh public hospitals fail to provide comprehensive cancer care
item.description: Cancer patients across Sindh, including Karachi, face major difficulties because no single public hospital in the province offers full treatment under one roof. Patients have to move between multiple ...

item.title: Telegram Increases Data Sharing with Spanish Authorities Following Founder’s Arrest
item.description: Telegram, historically known for its strong stance on privacy and reluctance to cooperate with governments, has significantly increased its collaboration with Spanish authorities over the past year. A...

item.title: DOJ Returns Villanueva Flood Control Cases to Ombudsman for Review
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has formally endorsed the flood control-related cases involving former Senator Villanueva back to the Office of the Ombudsman. These cases, which have been under scruti...

item.title: Reform UK Leads in YouGov Poll Amid Methodology Debate
item.description: The latest YouGov/Sky News/Times voting intention poll shows Reform UK leading with 25%, followed by the Green Party at 19%, and both the Conservatives and Labour tied at 17%. The Liberal Democrats ar...

item.title: Nvidia Introduces NemoClaw to Improve Security in Its OpenClaw AI Platform
item.description: During Nvidia’s 2026 GTC keynote, the company unveiled NemoClaw, a new security-focused stack designed to make its autonomous AI agent platform, OpenClaw, safer and more reliable. According to Nvidia,...

item.title: Many Tanzanian secondary schools face shortage of science laboratories
item.description: About two out of every five government secondary schools in Tanzania do not have proper science labs, making it hard for students to do practical experiments in biology, chemistry, and physics. Accord...
